body_txt: This is usually done in flashbacks. These are rarely Braces of Orthodontic Overkill but instead are more normal braces made to show that the character is either Hollywood Homely or a nerd. To show that they are young, characters wearing these tend to be a Burger Fool . This trope also often overlaps with Nerd Glasses . Truth in Television to an extent. Even if Hollywood tends to play this up a bit, braces are a common part of growing up, with about four million Americans at any given time undergoing some sort of orthodontic treatment, and about eighty percent of them being between the ages of six and eighteen. Of course, in the real world, braces can be worn at any age. This means they're not just pubescent. However, in the USA, some health insurance companies only pay for braces for people under a certain age (early to mid-20s). That makes this trope due to the price tag of paying out of pocket. Related to Braces-Based Bullying , with which it may overlap.
